supply_chain.dependencies: Many products sold in stores are sourced in locations (particularly in China, India and southeastern Asia) other than the location in which they will be sold
supply_chain.risks: Potential disruptions in manufacturing and supply, transport availability and costs, tariffs and trade restrictions, compliance with labor and environmental laws, supply chain interruptions, strikes, product liability claims, intellectual property issues, concerns about environmental impact and human rights in sourcing countries

regulatory_risks: Tariffs, duties, border adjustment taxes, trade restrictions, sanctions, quotas and voluntary export restrictions on imported merchandise
Changes to international trade agreements or enforcement practices
Compliance with labor, environmental, supply chain, international trade and other laws in relevant countries
Compliance with product laws and regulations including product safety and labeling requirements
Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act compliance
Countering America's Adversaries Through Sanctions Act
General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in European Union
Pillar Two Model Rules defining global minimum tax (15% minimum rate for large multinational corporations)
Labor and employment practices regulations including pay transparency requirements
Consumer protection and product safety regulations
Environmental regulations including cap and trade, carbon taxes, emissions management systems
Internet regulations including e-commerce and electronic communications
FDA regulations (U.S. Food Safety Modernization Act)
Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (1% excise tax on stock repurchases)

sanction_risks: Widespread sanctions as a result of ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict
Continuation of widespread sanctions related to Russia-Ukraine conflict
Shipping disruptions in Red Sea and surrounding waterways due to Middle East conflict
